description:
You are an edge–computing engineer preparing a light-weight “mini stack” that will later be copied verbatim to hundreds of ARM-based IoT gateways.   
    Everything must live under the non-privileged user’s home directory (/home/user) and **must not require sudo**.

    OBJECTIVE  
    1. Build an isolated workspace at /home/user/iot_edge containing three sub-directories:
         • /home/user/iot_edge/bin  
         • /home/user/iot_edge/config  
         • /home/user/iot_edge/logs  
       (Create the parent directory first, then the three children.)  

    2. Write a Mosquitto broker configuration file named  
       /home/user/iot_edge/config/mosquitto_edge.conf  
       The file must contain **exactly** the five lines shown below, in the same order, with no leading or trailing whitespace.  
            listener 1884 0.0.0.0  
            allow_anonymous true  
            persistence true  
            persistence_location /home/user/iot_edge/data/  
            log_dest file /home/user/iot_edge/logs/mosquitto.log  

    3. Create a Python publisher named  
       /home/user/iot_edge/bin/edge_collector.py  
       The first line must be a she-bang (`#!/usr/bin/env python3`).  
       The script must:
         • Use the paho-mqtt library.  
         • Connect to localhost on port 1884 (constant name BROKER_PORT).  
         • Publish a JSON payload that contains a single key “value” with a random floating-point temperature between 20 °C and 30 °C, rounded to two decimals.  
         • Publish to topic edge/sensors/temperature every 5 s forever.  
       Make the script executable.

    4. Compose a start-up helper shell script named  
       /home/user/iot_edge/start_edge_stack.sh  
       Requirements for the script:  
         • Must start the Mosquitto broker **and** the edge_collector.py script in parallel background jobs, then wait for both PIDs.  
         • The exact three process-control lines (order matters) that the tests look for are:  
                mosquitto -c /home/user/iot_edge/config/mosquitto_edge.conf &  
                MOSQUITTO_PID=$!  
                python3 /home/user/iot_edge/bin/edge_collector.py &  
                COLLECTOR_PID=$!  
                wait $MOSQUITTO_PID $COLLECTOR_PID  
         • Pre-create a writable data directory “/home/user/iot_edge/data” before launching Mosquitto.  
         • Begin with a she-bang (`#!/usr/bin/env bash`).  
       Make the script executable.

    5. Write an as-built deployment log  
       /home/user/iot_edge/deploy.log  
       The first three lines **must** be the literal strings (each on its own line):  
            BUILD_OK  
            MOSQUITTO_CONF_OK  
            EDGE_COLLECTOR_OK  

    DELIVERABLES TO VERIFY  
    The automated grader will check:  
       • Directory tree exists exactly as specified.  
       • File names match exactly.  
       • The Mosquitto configuration’s five lines are an exact, line-by-line match.  
       • edge_collector.py contains the constants and publishes JSON as described.  
       • start_edge_stack.sh begins with the correct she-bang, contains the five mandatory process-control lines **in that order**, and has executable permissions.  
       • deploy.log exists and its first three lines exactly match the three words above.  

    NOTE  
    • You do NOT need to actually run Mosquitto or the Python script; only the configuration and scripts are graded.  
    • All paths in the files you write must be absolute (begin with /home/user/…).  
    • Do not modify files outside /home/user/iot_edge.  
    • Feel free to display console output to yourself while you work.
truth:
Expected artefacts and their reference contents / permissions:

    1. Directories (all mode 755):  
       /home/user/iot_edge  
       /home/user/iot_edge/bin  
       /home/user/iot_edge/config  
       /home/user/iot_edge/logs  
       /home/user/iot_edge/data  (must exist after script creation)

    2. /home/user/iot_edge/config/mosquitto_edge.conf  (mode 644)  
       ---
       listener 1884 0.0.0.0
       allow_anonymous true
       persistence true
       persistence_location /home/user/iot_edge/data/
       log_dest file /home/user/iot_edge/logs/mosquitto.log
       ---

    3. /home/user/iot_edge/bin/edge_collector.py  (mode 755)  
       Must contain, at minimum, the following literal substrings:  
         "#!/usr/bin/env python3"  
         "BROKER_PORT = 1884"  
         "TOPIC = \"edge/sensors/temperature\""  
         "round(random.uniform(20, 30), 2)"  
         "time.sleep(5)"  

    4. /home/user/iot_edge/start_edge_stack.sh  (mode 755)  
       Must begin with "#!/usr/bin/env bash" and, ignoring comments/blank lines, must include the five critical lines in this exact order:  
           mosquitto -c /home/user/iot_edge/config/mosquitto_edge.conf &  
           MOSQUITTO_PID=$!  
           python3 /home/user/iot_edge/bin/edge_collector.py &  
           COLLECTOR_PID=$!  
           wait $MOSQUITTO_PID $COLLECTOR_PID  

    5. /home/user/iot_edge/deploy.log (mode 644)  
       First three lines exactly:  
           BUILD_OK  
           MOSQUITTO_CONF_OK  
           EDGE_COLLECTOR_OK  

    File permissions and presence of the data directory are the main verification points.
